title: L2 mode — full audit
description: Same deal as 'BASIC mode — compare' (qty=50000, budget=₹420), declares L2_EXECUTIVE_REASONER as the expected seller-response-mode. To run in L2 mode set SELLER_RESPONSE_MODE=L2_EXECUTIVE_REASONER in A2A/js/src/agents/seller-agent/.env (this is the default for the rig) and restart the seller. In L2 mode the seller routes through the consultation-router (Treasury + Inventory + Logistics + Credit advisors in parallel), then runs the advisor-math-aggregator (effective floor, NBS midpoint, utility), then calls Gemini as executive with the full bundle, then applies the 3-layer guardrails (clamp + sanity-warning + defensive substitution). Audit will have a RICH 'l2' extras block with consultations[], tacticsTrace[], any mathOverrides[], any defensiveActions[]. Compare against 'BASIC mode — compare' to see the audit-side difference for an identical deal.

expectedOutcome.likely: 3-round close at ~₹375–₹395/unit (closing band similar to BASIC; the AUDIT is where the modes differ). Audit JSON: extras.l2.consultations[] has 3 entries with treasury/inventory/logistics/credit advisor data; tacticsTrace[] shows the math-aggregator outputs per round; mathOverrides[] populated if Gemini's price ever fell outside the guardrail band.
expectedOutcome.possible: May close in 2 rounds if Gemini is healthy and free-tier quota is fresh. usedFallback flag on tacticsTrace entries reveals which rounds used real LLM vs rule-based fallback.
expectedOutcome.failureMode: If SELLER_RESPONSE_MODE in .env is set to BASIC_SALES_QUOTING_1 instead of L2_EXECUTIVE_REASONER, the scenario will still run but the audit's extras.l2 will be empty {} (BASIC path doesn't write L2 extras). Check the UI Settings card's 'Active' mode before running.

declaredButDeferred: sellerIntent (other fields — seller acts on its own .env config)
